entities.name: BACILLUS PACIFICUS (AKS1A)
entities.description: An arsenic-tolerant bacterium isolated from contaminated groundwater with plant growth promoting properties

entities.name: ARSENIC CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of arsenic in groundwater environments

entities.name: WEST BENGAL, INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the Bacillus pacificus isolate was collected

entities.name: BIOREMEDIATION
entities.description: The process of using biological organisms to remove or neutralize pollutants from contaminated sites

entities.name: MULTIMETAL RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a strain to grow in the presence of multiple heavy metals

entities.name: ARSENATE
entities.description: An oxidized form of arsenic that the strain can tolerate

entities.name: ARSENITE
entities.description: A reduced form of arsenic that the strain can tolerate

entities.name: ARSC
entities.description: A gene associated with arsenic resistance

entities.name: ARSB
entities.description: A gene associated with arsenic resistance

entities.name: ARSR
entities.description: A gene associated with arsenic resistance

entities.name: RAST
entities.description: Rapid Annotation using Subsystem Technology used for genome annotation

entities.name: GENOMIC ISLANDS
entities.description: Large segments of DNA acquired by horizontal gene transfer that contribute to adaptation

entities.name: PHAGE VIRUS GENOMIC PARTS
entities.description: Viral genomic sequences integrated into the bacterial genome

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL STRESS
entities.description: External pressures in the environment that organisms must adapt to

entities.name: INDOLE ACETIC ACID
entities.description: A plant growth hormone produced by the bacterium

entities.name: GIBBERELLIC ACID
entities.description: A plant growth hormone produced by the bacterium

entities.name: PROLINE
entities.description: An amino acid produced by the bacterium that aids in stress tolerance

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The ability to convert atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form for plants

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH PROMOTION
entities.description: The ability of a microorganism to enhance plant growth and development

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The plant species used to test the growth enhancement properties of the bacterium

entities.name: ARSENIC STRESS
entities.description: The physiological stress imposed on plants by arsenic toxicity

entities.name: ARSENITE OXIDATION
entities.description: The biochemical process of converting arsenite to arsenate

entities.name: ARSENIC ADSORPTION
entities.description: The ability of the bacterium to bind arsenic to its cell surface

entities.name: XRF
entities.description: X-ray Fluorescence spectroscopy used to confirm arsenic adsorption

entities.name: FTIR
entities.description: Fourier Transform Infrared spectroscopy used to confirm arsenic adsorption

entities.name: EDS
entities.description: Energy Dispersive X-ray spectroscopic analysis used to confirm arsenic adsorption

entities.name: GENOMIC COMPARISON
entities.description: The process of comparing the genomes of different strains of the same species

entities.name: BIOFERTILIZER
entities.description: A substance containing living microorganisms that increases soil fertility

entities.name: BIOSTIMULANT
entities.description: An agent that stimulates plant growth and productivity
